reforms.description: The government has launched initiatives to promote research and development (R&D) spending, focusing on enhancing the financial sector's competitiveness and stimulating spending in areas where Luxembourg has existing strengths, such as logistics.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing R&D spending, the initiatives are expected to improve competitiveness and innovation in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiatives represent a significant but narrower reform aimed at boosting R&D spending.
reforms.source_quote: Another avenue to pursue this objective (and to bolster productivity growth) is the government’s programme to stimulate spending on research and development (R&D) with a focus on areas where Luxembourg has some strength already, such as logistics.

reforms.description: The government has presented a draft law to simplify work and entry permits for highly skilled workers and scientists, merging work and residence permits into one and allowing for special residence permits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying work permits is expected to attract skilled workers, enhancing the talent pool for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The draft law has been officially announced but is not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The draft law represents a significant reform aimed at improving the human capital landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government has presented a draft law to parliament to simplify work and entry permits for workers and entrepreneurs.

reforms.description: Luxembourg has implemented several reforms to modernise hospitals and improve quality, including linking part of the budget funding to quality standards assessed by an external committee.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Modernising hospitals and linking funding to quality standards is expected to enhance healthcare services and efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented since 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant change in the healthcare infrastructure aimed at improving service quality.
reforms.source_quote: Since 1998, several reforms have been implemented to modernise hospitals and to increase their quality.

reforms.description: A scientific council has been established in the health sector to identify and diffuse best practices for high-quality health services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a scientific council is expected to enhance knowledge transfer and improve healthcare practices.
reforms.status_evidence: The scientific council has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the council represents a significant effort to improve health service quality through knowledge diffusion.
reforms.source_quote: A scientific council has been established in the health sector to identify and diffuse best practices for high-quality health services.
